title: Bound and compact fresh open-PR observation for what-now
goal: Reduce what-now latency and output volume without weakening the fresh fail-closed open pull-request claim guard.

acceptance.assertion: Distinct GitHub repositories are observed concurrently through the existing fresh gh pr list path; no cache or search-index snapshot weakens claim safety.
Concurrency is bounded and configurable, duplicate repository observations are deduplicated, and one repository failure remains a fail-closed blocker only for affected resources.
what-now offers a compact projection that retains decision and blocker truth while omitting high-volume evidence details; full output remains available by default.
A live before/after benchmark records frontier latency and compact JSON size on the exact reviewed head.
Focused tests and complete Bureau validation pass on the exact reviewed head.

metadata.safety_boundary: Do not cache or use eventually consistent search results for claim-time PR truth. Preserve per-repository fail-closed diagnostics and configured PR count cap.
does_not_establish: absence of GitHub API latency variance
